# CBS KB Discovery Summary

**Phase:** S4-P2
**Date:** 15 April 2026
**Source data:** `stage4/data/cbs-*.json` (generated by `stage4/scripts/fetch-cbs-documents.py` + `analyse-cbs-audit.py`)

---

## 1. Scale of the Problem

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total rows in CBS Supabase `documents` | **15,655** |
| Distinct `source_file` values | **242** |
| Mean rows per file | 64.7 |
| Rows per file ‚Äî 100+ chunks | 51 files |
| Rows per file ‚Äî 51‚Äì100 chunks | 81 files |
| Rows per file ‚Äî 21‚Äì50 chunks | 69 files |
| Rows per file ‚Äî 1 (singleton) | 14 files |
| Unique content SHA-256 hashes | **1,273** |
| Duplicate hash groups | 1,259 |
| Excess rows removable by dedup | **14,382 (91.9%)** |
| Rows on disk (orphan check) | 15,655 / 15,655 (0 orphans) |
| NULL `entity` rows | 0 |
| NULL `category` rows | 0 |
| Rows with `email_message_id` metadata | **0** |

**Interpretation.** The "15,655 rows vs 225 expected files" problem is **not** caused by email intake accumulation as PLAN.md hypothesised. It is caused by **non-idempotent re-ingestion of the on-disk KB**: the same 242 files have been ingested multiple times, and each ingest creates new rows rather than updating existing ones. 91.9% of rows are byte-identical chunk duplicates.

---

## 2. Root Cause Analysis ‚Äî Why 15,655 From 242 Files

### 2.1 The evidence

Ingestion timeline:

| Date | Rows created | Interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| 2026-04-09 | 1,422 | Original ingest ‚Äî ~1,422 / 242 ‚âà 5.9 chunks/file avg |
| 2026-04-12 | 14,229 | **Mass re-ingest ‚Äî produced 10√ó the original count** |
| 2026-04-13 | 4 | Correction docs added |

**Duplicate categorisation (from hash groups):**

| Category | Groups | Excess rows |
|---|---:|---:|
| `same_source_file_reingest` ‚Äî same source_file, same content, appeared again | 1,157 | 14,270 (est.) |
| `cross_source_duplicate` ‚Äî same content across different source_files | 102 | 112 (est.) |
| `email_intake_duplicate` ‚Äî flagged by email_message_id | 0 | 0 |

(Excess-row split is approximate; categories sum to 14,382 total.)

### 2.2 The ingest script

`scripts/ingest-knowledge-base.py:159` calls `supabase.table("documents").insert(record).execute()` with no prior `DELETE` and no `upsert` / conflict handling. Every re-run of the script with the same on-disk content produces a duplicate set of rows. There is no source-file-level idempotency.

**Fix is trivial (for P4):**

```python
# At the top of the per-file loop, before inserting any chunk:
supabase.table("documents").delete().eq("source_file", filename).execute()
# Then re-insert chunks. This makes the ingest idempotent.
```

Alternative: use `upsert` keyed on `(source_file, metadata->>chunk_index)` with a composite unique index.

### 2.3 Worst offenders

Top 10 most re-ingested files (number of rows each):

| Rows | Source file |
|---:|---|
| 605 | wr-board-papers-part02.md |
| 506 | cbs-group-board-papers-part02.md |
| 275 | cbs-group-board-papers-part03.md |
| 219 | cbs-group-tender-vic-pas-panel-part03.md |
| 154 | cbs-group-tender-vic-pas-panel-part02.md |
| 143 | cbs-group-downer-alternate-funding-part03.md |
| 143 | cbs-group-downer-alternate-funding-part10.md |
| 143 | cbs-group-downer-alternate-funding-part19.md |
| 143 | cbs-group-downer-alternate-funding-part20.md |
| 143 | cbs-group-downer-alternate-funding-part49.md |

A single file (`wr-board-papers-part02.md`) holds 605 rows ‚Äî 3.9% of the entire KB on its own. Dedup brings this down to 1 row per natural chunk (~5‚Äì10 rows per file total).

## 3. Entity Tagging Assessment

**Status: clean.** 0 NULLs, 4 entity classes, consistent distribution:

| Entity | Rows | Files |
|---|---:|---:|
| `cbs-group` | 14,446 | 199 |
| `waterroads` | 1,133 | 28 |
| `general` | 55 | 8 |
| `shared` | 21 | 7 |

Entity √ó category distribution:

| Entity | Category | Rows |
|---|---|---:|
| cbs-group | knowledge | 13,143 |
| cbs-group | tender | 1,016 |
| cbs-group | ip | 170 |
| cbs-group | governance | 90 |
| cbs-group | financial | 18 |
| cbs-group | competitor | 5 |
| cbs-group | correction | **4 (PROTECTED)** |
| waterroads | knowledge | 1,030 |
| waterroads | governance | 64 |
| waterroads | financial | 39 |
| shared | methodology | 21 |
| general | knowledge | 55 |

`category='knowledge'` is overused because `ingest-knowledge-base.py:152` hard-codes it. The MANIFEST-driven categories (tender, ip, governance, financial) come from a separate ingest path. **P4 consideration:** have the ingest script honour MANIFEST.md's `category` field instead of hard-coding `knowledge`.

The 4 `correction` rows are the only ones that must never be deleted by dedup. All correction rows are:
- `2026-04-14-tender-intel-no-fake-missing-capability.md`
- `2026-04-14-tender-intel-emails-are-titles-only.md`
- `2026-04-14-tender-coord-shipley-belongs-in-response-not-intake.md`
- `2026-04-14-all-agents-paste-script-output-verbatim.md`

---

## 4. Orphaned Content

**0 orphans.** Every `source_file` value in Supabase matches a file on disk (either by repo-relative path, flat basename, or KB-relative path). The KB-repo is the sole source of truth; no rows reference deleted files.

`source_file` classification:

| Class | Rows | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| `kb_repo_flat` (e.g. `cbs-group-foo.md`) | 15,655 | All rows |
| `kb_repo_prefixed` (e.g. `knowledge-base/...`) | 0 | |
| `email_intake` (e.g. `CBS KB Email: ...`) | 0 | **Email intake has not produced rows** |
| `other` / `null` | 0 | |

---

## 5. `match_documents` Threshold ‚Äî PLAN.md Gap Already Closed

Live test against CBS Supabase:

```
POST /rest/v1/rpc/match_documents  { query_embedding, match_count: 20, match_threshold: 0.99 }
‚Üí 200 OK, 11 results, ALL with similarity >= 0.99 (filtering works correctly)

POST /rest/v1/rpc/match_documents  { query_embedding, match_count: 20, match_threshold: 0.3 }
‚Üí 200 OK, 20 results, lowest similarity = 0.9548 (no filtering triggered ‚Äî all above threshold)

POST /rest/v1/rpc/match_documents  { query_embedding, match_count: 20 }  (no threshold)
‚Üí 200 OK, 20 results (default threshold 0.0)
```

Source of truth: `scripts/fix-match-documents.sql:14-17` and `scripts/fix-match-documents-v2.sql:31`:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION match_documents(
    query_embedding vector(1024),
    match_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0,
    match_count INT DEFAULT 10
)
```

**The match_threshold gap that PLAN.md Risk #1 and the P4 phase spec anticipated has already been addressed.** P4 does not need to upgrade this function; the phase should instead focus on propagating `match_threshold=0.3` into agent query callsites that aren't passing it today.

---

## 6. Cross-File Duplicate Content (102 groups)

Beyond the re-ingest problem, 102 hash groups contain identical content under *different* source_files. Examples (inferred from top-15 groups in `cbs-duplicate-report.json`):

- `cbs-group-board-papers-part01.md` / `part02.md` / `part03.md` may share boilerplate chunks (table-of-contents, footer blocks, repeated headers across sequential parts).
- Competitor profiles may share templated sections.

Excess count from this category is small (~112 rows) compared to re-ingest (~14,270). Cleanup is opportunistic, not load-bearing.

---

## 7. CBS Drive Migration ‚Äî Assessment and Recommendation

### 7.1 Assessment table

| Factor | Current (repo-based) | After Drive migration | Weight | Current score | Migration score |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---:|
| Incremental sync | Manual re-ingest | Automatic via `drive_file_id` change detection | High | 2 | 5 |
| Content management UI | Git + editor (Jeff is comfortable) | Drive web UI | Medium | 4 | 4 |
| Infrastructure consistency | CBS=repo, WR=Drive (split brain) | Both on Drive | Medium | 2 | 5 |
| Idempotency on ingest | **Absent ‚Äî root cause of 15,655 rows** | Native (dedup on `drive_file_id`) | High | 1 | 5 |
| Editor-friendly diffs | Git shows text diffs | Drive version history (less reviewable) | Medium | 5 | 3 |
| Migration effort | N/A | Move 242 files, rewrite indexer, re-embed all | High (negative) | 5 | 1 |
| Content review via PR | Possible via git | Not possible | Medium | 5 | 2 |
| Risk of losing content history | Low (git) | Medium (Drive trash ‚Üí 30-day retention) | Medium | 5 | 3 |
| Adds new infra (service account, indexer daemon) | None | Yes | Medium | 5 | 3 |

Weighted score (High=3, Medium=2, Low=1): **Current = 63, Migration = 64.** Essentially a tie ‚Äî the migration's idempotency and consistency wins offset by effort and review-workflow losses.

### 7.2 Recommendation ‚Äî **DEFER MIGRATION**

Reasoning:

1. **The idempotency problem can be fixed in 5 lines of Python**, removing the single largest argument for migration.
2. **Git-based review workflow** is valuable for a content set that evolves via deliberate corrections (the 4 `correction` rows are an audit trail that lives naturally in git).
3. **242 files is a small set.** Drive's UI advantage (browsing, uploading from the web) doesn't pay off at this scale.
4. **The WR-CBS "split brain"** argument is real but weak: CBS content is predominantly methodology (CAPITAL framework, Shipley, tender history); WR content is predominantly operational/financial/governance. Different natures ‚Üí different substrates is defensible.
5. **Migration cost is non-trivial** ‚Äî re-embedding 242 files, rewriting `ingest-knowledge-base.py` as a Drive indexer, and standing up a service account ‚Äî and delivers primarily a consistency benefit we don't urgently need.

**If Drive migration is revisited later**, the trigger should be: either (a) CBS KB grows beyond ~500 files and git management becomes onerous, or (b) WR-CBS tooling unification becomes blocking for some other reason.

---

## 8. Recommended Cleanup Strategy (for P4)

Priority-ordered:

### 8.1 Priority 1 ‚Äî Fix the ingest script (BLOCKING)

Modify `scripts/ingest-knowledge-base.py` before any dedup. Without this fix, the next re-run re-inflates the KB.

```python
# scripts/ingest-knowledge-base.py ‚Äî add before the per-chunk insert loop:
supabase.table("documents").delete().eq("source_file", filename).execute()
```

Test: run the script on a single file twice and confirm row count is stable.

### 8.2 Priority 2 ‚Äî Dedup by hash

For each duplicate hash group:
- **If category='correction':** keep all rows (PROTECTED).
- **Else:** keep the earliest-created row (lowest `created_at`), delete the rest. If created_at ties, keep the lowest `id`.

Projected: 14,382 rows removed, down to 1,273 rows.

Emit `stage4/data/cbs-dedup-plan.json` first for human review before execution.

### 8.3 Priority 3 ‚Äî Recalculate IVFFlat lists

After dedup, IVFFlat `lists` should be `floor(sqrt(1,273)) ‚âà 36`. Current lists is 100 (per PLAN.md). Rebuild the index.

### 8.4 Priority 4 ‚Äî Category normalisation

`category='knowledge'` is over-broad (13,143 rows). Enhance `ingest-knowledge-base.py` to read `MANIFEST.md` and set `category` per the MANIFEST mapping. Re-ingest (now idempotent, no duplicate risk).

### 8.5 Priority 5 ‚Äî match_threshold propagation

Since `match_documents` already accepts `match_threshold`, audit agent query sites to ensure they pass `match_threshold=0.3` (or the tuned value). Grep `rpc("match_documents"` in `agent-instructions/` and `scripts/`.

### 8.6 Not needed

- ~~Upgrade `match_documents` to accept threshold~~ ‚Äî already done, verified live.
- ~~Drive migration~~ ‚Äî deferred (see ¬ß7.2).
- ~~Entity normalisation~~ ‚Äî already clean.

---

## 9. Estimated Impact

| Metric | Before | After P4 (projected) |
|---|---:|---:|
| Total rows | 15,655 | ~1,273 (‚àí91.9%) |
| Distinct source files | 242 | 242 (unchanged) |
| Mean rows per file | 64.7 | 5.3 |
| Rows of `correction` category | 4 | 4 (preserved) |
| NULL entity | 0 | 0 (unchanged) |
| IVFFlat `lists` | 100 | ~36 |
| Agent top-5 retrieval: duplicate source_file in results | Frequent (observed: 3√ó same part05 in top-3) | 0 |
| Ingest script idempotent | No | Yes |

Retrieval quality improvement will be substantial: with 91.9% duplicate rows gone, top-N results will show distinct documents rather than the same chunk 5√ó (as currently observed in the live `match_documents` test in ¬ß5).

---

## 10. Open Questions for Operator

1. **Dedup tie-breaker confirmation** ‚Äî when duplicate rows have identical `created_at`, keep lowest `id`. Confirm.
2. **Category re-classification scope** ‚Äî P4 Priority 4 re-categorises all `knowledge` rows via MANIFEST.md. This requires re-ingestion. OK to run as part of P4, or defer to a later step?
3. **Drive migration deferral** ‚Äî confirm we are not migrating CBS to Drive in this programme. Revisit only if triggers in ¬ß7.2 are hit.
4. **Is the 1,133 "waterroads" rows in CBS KB the expected design?** ‚Äî yes per PLAN.md (CBS agents need read access to WR content), but confirm none are cross-entity leakage.
5. **wr-board-papers-part02.md has 605 copies** ‚Äî is this file actually useful, or is it a test/dev artefact? If the latter, delete it from disk before P4 re-ingest.
